description: Single source of truth for pi security. Edit this when a layer changes; then run ./security/check.sh to regenerate SECURITY.md and re-run all tests.

layers.name: Bash sandbox (sandbox-exec)
layers.purpose: Sandbox the bash tool's child processes via macOS sandbox-exec. Blocks writes outside allowWrite, blocks reads of denyRead, restricts network to allowedDomains.
knownGaps: Raw-IP egress bypasses domain allowlist (sandbox-runtime matches by hostname only) — XFAIL, accepted v1 risk

layers.name: In-process tool guard
layers.purpose: Catch what sandbox-exec can't: the in-process read/write/edit/fetch_content/web_search/get_search_content tools. Same policy file as L1.
knownGaps: Live end-to-end tests via the actual tools are manual today (no in-session test harness in pi yet)
fetch_content redirects are not re-checked against the allowlist

layers.name: Subagent posture
layers.purpose: Stricter network policy when ctx.hasUI === false (subagents, -p mode, JSON mode). Filesystem deny rules apply unchanged in every context.

config.note: research-only uses a transcript heuristic to identify librarian/scout/researcher agents. Best-effort; v2 should plumb agent identity through ctx.

tests.command: echo 'manual: set subagent.network=deny in sandbox.json, run pi -p "fetch https://example.com"; expect block'

knownGaps: ctx.hasUI=false also catches legitimate scripted runs, not just subagents — that's why default is 'allow'
No first-class agent identity in ctx

layers.name: Browser gate (chrome_devtools_*)
layers.purpose: Per-session ctx.ui.confirm for mutating chrome_devtools_* tools (navigate_page, click, fill, evaluate_script, etc.). Read-only ops (snapshot, screenshot) stay open. Subagents always denied.

uxPolish.what: sandbox/index.ts now scans merged stdout+stderr for EPERM/EACCES, so `cmd 2>&1 | head` still triggers the hint.

Hint extracts the offending path and recognizes tool config dirs (~/.config/, ~/.kube/, ~/.docker/, ~/.netrc, ~/.aws/, ~/.npmrc, ~/.gitconfig). Suggests a project-local .pi/sandbox.json snippet with the path pre-filled. Says explicitly 'this is the pi sandbox, NOT macOS TCC' so the model stops misdiagnosing.
Ask-tier UX: when sandbox blocks AND ctx.hasUI, prompt 'Retry without sandbox? [y/N/always-for-this-cwd]'. 'always' writes path into <cwd>/.pi/sandbox.json. Avoids per-command-prompt antipattern.

docs.role: Auto-generated current-state overview. DO NOT EDIT — regenerated by security/check.sh.
Long-form intent / threat model / acceptance criteria. Hand-edited.
Deep-dive: configs, code patches, setup commands. Hand-edited.
Decision log: why this design, what we accepted as v1 risk. Hand-edited.
Layer-by-layer test matrix + manual-test recipes. Hand-edited.
THE source of truth for layer status. Edit this when a layer changes.
